cases.name: Sorrells v. United States
cases.holding: Entrapment is a valid defense; it arises when government officials implant the criminal design in the mind of a person who had no previous disposition to commit the offense and then lure that "otherwise innocent" person into committing it.

cases.name: Sherman v. United States
cases.holding: Entrapment is established as a matter of law when the government, through its informant, implants the criminal design in an otherwise-unwilling person and induces the crime; the defendant's lack of predisposition was clear (he was a recovering addict pressured by a fellow patient).

cases.name: United States v. Russell
cases.holding: There is no entrapment where the defendant was predisposed, even though a government agent supplied a difficult-to-obtain but legal ingredient for the crime; the Court (5-4) declined to overturn the predisposition test and rejected the proposed objective test.

cases.name: Jacobson v. United States
cases.holding: Where the government induces the crime, it must prove the defendant was predisposed to commit it INDEPENDENT of, and PRIOR TO, the government's inducement; 26 months of government solicitation that itself created the predisposition means the prosecution failed as a matter of law, so entrapment is established.

quotes.text: the proposition that the accused must be predisposed prior to contact with law enforcement officers is so firmly established that the Government conceded the point at oral argument

cases.name: Hampton v. United States
cases.holding: Neither the entrapment defense nor the Due Process Clause bars conviction of a PREDISPOSED defendant even where a government agent supplied the contraband (heroin) the defendant then sold; his conceded predisposition defeated the entrapment defense.

cases.name: Mathews v. United States
cases.holding: A defendant who denies one or more elements of the charged crime is nonetheless entitled to an entrapment instruction whenever there is sufficient evidence from which a reasonable jury could find entrapment.

quotes.text: We hold that even if the defendant denies one or more elements of the crime, he is entitled to an entrapment instruction whenever there is sufficient evidence from which a reasonable jury could find entrapment.

cases.treatment_note: Current; defendant need not admit the acts to raise entrapment.

note: Phase-3 FINAL authoring manifest: packet(manifest+deck) + P3 gap verifications + marquee quotes + good-law.
